Note: This article is a part of the Cognitive Governance Matrix: 25 Design Patterns for Production-Grade Agentic AI
Not a Medium member? Read full article here.
⚠️ The Problem: The “Babbling” Swarm
When developers first build a Multi-Agent System, they naturally assume the agents should talk to each other the same way humans do: via open-ended chat messages.
However, Large Language Models are inherently verbose. If a “Financial Agent” asks a “Database Agent” for Q3 revenue, the Database Agent might respond with: “Hello! I would be happy to help you with that. According to my SQL query, the Q3 revenue is $45,000. Let me know if you need anything else!” The result? Context Rot and Parsing Failures.

• The Token Tax: Conversational filler consumes massive amounts of the system’s context window and drives up API costs for absolutely no business value.

• Quadratic Overhead: In a peer-to-peer mesh architecture, agents communicate directly through message passing, but this increases coordination overhead as the number of potential communication paths grows quadratically.

• The Parsing Nightmare: Downstream agents expect clean data to make decisions. When they receive conversational paragraphs instead of raw numbers, their internal logic breaks, causing the entire workflow to crash.
